name: Mlir.Loc

unions.name: Loc
unions.comment: A source code location within a named file with start and end positions defined as column and row.
cases: { name : String.String, start : Mlir.Loc.Pos, end : Mlir.Loc.Pos }

values.name: combine
values.comment:
Combines two locations into one larger location that runs from the earliest start point to latest
end point of the two locations.

It is assumed that the two locations refer to the same named file, the actual file name returned will be
`a.name`.

values.name: unknown
values.comment: The default unknown location, which is file "unknown" at (0, 0) to (0, 0)

name: Mlir.Mlir

unions.name: DenseF64Value
unions.comment: Dense F64 values, such as tensors.

unions.name: MlirAttr
unions.comment: Attributes are constant, known-value pieces of metadata attached to operations and functions.

unions.name: MlirRegion
unions.comment: A region, which is a sequence of basic blocks with scoping.
cases: { entry : Mlir.Mlir.MlirBlock, blocks : OrderedDict.OrderedDict String.String Mlir.Mlir.MlirBlock }

unions.name: MlirType
unions.comment:
MLIR uses an open type system, meaning there is no fixed, hardcoded list of all valid types.
Instead, types are defined within specific dialects, which are modular extensions to the core MLIR
framework.

A hard-coded set of common types is provided here.
cases: { inputs : List.List Mlir.Mlir.MlirType, results : List.List Mlir.Mlir.MlirType }

unions.name: OpBuilder
unions.comment: An opaque builder representing a MlirOp under construction.

aliases.name: DenseF64
aliases.comment: Dense F64 values, such as tensors.

aliases.name: MlirBlock
aliases.type: { args : List.List ( String.String, Mlir.Mlir.MlirType ), body : List.List Mlir.Mlir.MlirOp, terminator : Mlir.Mlir.MlirOp }

aliases.name: MlirModule
aliases.comment: A module which is a list of MLIR operations, often functions with regions.

aliases.name: MlirOp
aliases.type: { name : String.String, id : String.String, operands : List.List String.String, results : List.List ( String.String, Mlir.Mlir.MlirType ), attrs : Dict.Dict String.String Mlir.Mlir.MlirAttr, regions : List.List Mlir.Mlir.MlirRegion, isTerminator : Basics.Bool, loc : Mlir.Loc.Loc, successors : List.List String.String }

aliases.name: OpBuilderFns
aliases.comment: A set of builder functions for creation `MlirOp`s.
aliases.type: { withOperands : List.List String.String -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e, withResults : List.List ( String.String, Mlir.Mlir.MlirType ) -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e, withAttrs : Dict.Dict String.String Mlir.Mlir.MlirAttr -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e, withRegions : List.List Mlir.Mlir.MlirRegion -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e, isTerminator : Basics.Bool -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e, withLoc : Mlir.Loc.Loc -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e, withSuccessors : List.List String.String -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e, build : Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e -> ( e, Mlir.Mlir.MlirOp ) }

values.name: mlirOp
values.comment:
Creates a builder for a named MlirOp with a generated id that is unique within a given environment.
This is designed for convenience of allowing an id generation function to be specified, and to pass
through a new environment (with a particular id consumed).

The remaining parameters can be filled in using the builder functions in OpBuilderFn.
values.type: (e -> ( e, String.String )) -> e -> String.String -> Mlir.Mlir.OpBuilder e

values.name: opBuilder
values.comment: An implementation of the builder functions.

name: Mlir.Pretty
comment:
Mlir.Pretty provides a pretty printer for the Mlir.Mlir model that will output MLIR in text format
in the standard format.

This implementation does not currently allow custom formats.

@docs ppModule

values.name: ppModule
values.comment: Pretty prints an MLIR module in the standard format.
